_comment: Static evaluation rubric for the synthetic CUDA benchmark suite. Keyed by the 'scenario' field in runtime ground-truth JSONs. Used by PerfAdvisor to score hypothesis quality: did the advisor identify the right bottleneck and recommend the right fixes?

nsys_signature: CPU timeline dense with cudaLaunchKernel API calls; GPU timeline shows a barcode of very short kernel executions separated by idle gaps
GPU utilization is near zero despite high CPU activity
Each kernel active period is shorter than the preceding idle gap (launch latency > execution time)

suggestions.action: Use CUDA Graphs to record the launch sequence once and replay with near-zero per-launch CPU overhead
suggestions.mechanism: cudaGraphCreate / cudaGraphInstantiate / cudaGraphLaunch
suggestions.rationale: Replaying a graph avoids the driver overhead of N individual kernel submissions; ideal when the same sequence of kernels fires repeatedly

suggestions.action: Fuse multiple small kernels into a single larger kernel so each launch does more work
suggestions.mechanism: Manual kernel fusion or template-based work batching
suggestions.rationale: Reduces total launch count proportionally; each GPU active window grows, eliminating idle gaps

suggestions.action: For producer-consumer pipelines, use persistent kernels that loop internally rather than re-launching
suggestions.mechanism: Grid-stride loop with device-side work queues or cooperative groups
suggestions.rationale: Eliminates re-launch entirely; the kernel stays resident and picks up new work without CPU involvement

nsys_signature: CPU timeline alternates between cudaLaunchKernel and cudaStreamSynchronize API calls
GPU is idle during each cudaStreamSynchronize; CPU thread is blocked waiting for GPU drain
No concurrent GPU activity across streams; effective GPU utilization is well below 50%

suggestions.action: Remove per-launch cudaStreamSynchronize; synchronize only at genuine data-dependency boundaries (e.g., before a CPU read of GPU-produced data)
suggestions.mechanism: Identify critical-path syncs with Nsight Systems timeline; replace non-critical syncs with cudaEventRecord / cudaStreamWaitEvent
suggestions.rationale: Breaks the CPU-GPU serialization loop; GPU can stay resident across multiple launches while CPU queues the next batch

suggestions.action: Use CUDA events to express fine-grained dependencies between streams without blocking the CPU thread
suggestions.mechanism: cudaEventRecord on the producing stream; cudaStreamWaitEvent on the consuming stream
suggestions.rationale: GPU-side dependency tracking lets the driver sequence work correctly without stalling the CPU

suggestions.action: If the CPU genuinely needs results, batch the synchronization at the end of a full pipeline stage rather than after each kernel
suggestions.mechanism: cudaStreamSynchronize or cudaDeviceSynchronize at stage boundaries only
suggestions.rationale: Reduces sync frequency from O(N kernels) to O(N stages); amortizes the stall cost

nsys_signature: Timeline dominated by cudaMemcpyAsync H2D and D2H events; MemCpy rows are densely occupied
Compute kernels are visible between transfers but their duration is much shorter than the flanking transfer bars
Effective PCIe bandwidth is near the device maximum; GPU compute SMs are underutilized relative to the transfer engine

suggestions.action: Increase arithmetic intensity: do significantly more computation per byte transferred so GPU time dominates transfer time
suggestions.mechanism: Increase work_iters or restructure the algorithm to operate on data while it is resident on the GPU across multiple passes
suggestions.rationale: Shifts the bottleneck from the PCIe link to the compute SMs; transfers become a smaller fraction of total time

suggestions.action: Keep data GPU-resident across iterations to eliminate H2D/D2H round-trips entirely
suggestions.mechanism: Allocate output buffers on the device; only transfer final results back to host
suggestions.rationale: Eliminates D2H + subsequent H2D for the same buffer; reduces total PCIe traffic proportionally

suggestions.action: Use pinned (page-locked) host memory to maximize PCIe throughput if transfers are unavoidable
suggestions.mechanism: cudaMallocHost / cudaFreeHost for host-side staging buffers
suggestions.rationale: Pinned memory allows DMA to proceed at full PCIe bandwidth without OS page-faulting

nsys_signature: Strictly sequential per-chunk H2D → kernel → D2H pattern; no two of these activities overlap in the timeline
All streams appear to execute in series; MemCpy and Kernel rows never have simultaneous activity
Total runtime is approximately N_chunks × (H2D_time + kernel_time + D2H_time) rather than approaching max(sum_H2D, sum_kernel, sum_D2H)

suggestions.action: Pipeline chunks across streams: while stream A computes chunk K, stream B transfers chunk K+1 H2D and stream C transfers chunk K-1 D2H
suggestions.mechanism: Use at least 3 CUDA streams (transfer-in, compute, transfer-out); issue all operations asynchronously
suggestions.rationale: Hides transfer latency behind compute; ideal throughput approaches max(PCIe bandwidth, compute throughput)

suggestions.action: Eliminate any implicit synchronization that collapses the pipeline
suggestions.mechanism: Audit for cudaDeviceSynchronize, cudaStreamSynchronize, and host-pinned memory faults between chunk submissions
suggestions.rationale: A single implicit sync forces all in-flight work to drain before the next chunk begins, destroying the pipeline

suggestions.action: Use cudaMemcpyAsync (not cudaMemcpy) and ensure host buffers are page-locked so async copies can proceed concurrently with kernels
suggestions.mechanism: cudaMallocHost for host staging buffers; cudaMemcpyAsync with non-default stream
suggestions.rationale: cudaMemcpy is blocking by default; only the async variant can overlap with kernel execution on a separate stream

p2p_staged.display_name: Intra-node P2P with unnecessary host staging
nsys_signature: Per-halo-exchange triplet visible: D2H cudaMemcpy → MPI_Sendrecv → H2D cudaMemcpy
GPU is idle during MPI_Sendrecv; data is sitting on the host
PCIe utilization appears twice per exchange (once for D2H, once for H2D); total transfer volume is 2× the halo size

suggestions.action: Pass device pointers directly to MPI_Sendrecv (CUDA-aware MPI); eliminate the explicit D2H/H2D copies
suggestions.mechanism: Remove cudaMemcpy D2H/H2D calls flanking MPI_Sendrecv; pass dev_buf directly to MPI; ensure MPICH_GPU_SUPPORT_ENABLED=1
suggestions.rationale: GTL handles the intra-node path via CUDA IPC or peer memcpy; the CPU is no longer on the data path

suggestions.action: For NVLink-connected GPUs on the same node, enable peer access so MPI can use direct device-to-device copies
suggestions.mechanism: cudaDeviceEnablePeerAccess between all rank pairs before MPI initialization
suggestions.rationale: NVLink bandwidth is 600 GB/s vs 32 GB/s PCIe; a single peer copy replaces two PCIe transfers

suggestions.action: If using CUDA-aware MPI is not feasible, at least overlap the host staging of the next halo with the compute of the current step (double-buffering)
suggestions.mechanism: Use two halo buffers and alternate; stage buffer B while computing with the data in buffer A
suggestions.rationale: Hides the D2H + MPI + H2D latency behind useful work even without GPU-Direct

nsys_signature: Fast ranks show brief GPU kernel activity followed by a long CPU wait inside MPI_Barrier
Slow ranks (higher rank index) show proportionally longer GPU kernel execution before entering MPI_Barrier
The barrier wait time for rank 0 is approximately (max_rank_work - rank_0_work); wasted time is visible as a CPU-idle gap in the Nsight Systems MPI row

suggestions.action: Balance work across ranks so each rank spends the same time in GPU compute before the barrier
suggestions.mechanism: Profile per-rank GPU kernel duration; equalize work_iters or partition the problem domain proportionally
suggestions.rationale: Reduces the barrier wait time to near zero; total throughput is limited by the slowest rank

suggestions.action: Replace MPI_Barrier with MPI_Ibarrier (non-blocking) so fast ranks can queue follow-on work while waiting
suggestions.mechanism: MPI_Ibarrier + MPI_Wait; queue independent GPU work between the two calls on fast ranks
suggestions.rationale: Fast ranks overlap their barrier wait with useful computation instead of spinning

suggestions.action: Restructure the algorithm to avoid a global synchronization point on the critical path
suggestions.mechanism: Replace global barrier with point-to-point synchronization (MPI_Send/Recv or MPI_Isend/Irecv) between dependent rank pairs only
suggestions.rationale: Scope of synchronization matches the actual data dependency; ranks not dependent on each other never stall

nsys_signature: Per-reduction triplet visible on each rank: D2H cudaMemcpy → MPI_Allreduce → H2D cudaMemcpy
GPU is idle during MPI_Allreduce; both PCIe directions are fully utilized flanking the collective
Two full-array PCIe transfers (D2H + H2D) per collective call; for large buffers this dominates reduction time

suggestions.action: Pass device pointers directly to MPI_Allreduce (CUDA-aware MPI) to eliminate the D2H/H2D staging
suggestions.mechanism: Remove cudaMemcpy D2H/H2D calls around MPI_Allreduce; pass dev_buf directly; ensure MPICH_GPU_SUPPORT_ENABLED=1
suggestions.rationale: GTL routes the collective through device memory; host is not on the data path

suggestions.action: Use NCCL ncclAllReduce for higher-bandwidth intra-node reductions and network-aware inter-node reductions
suggestions.mechanism: Replace MPI_Allreduce with ncclAllReduce; NCCL uses NVLink intra-node and RDMA inter-node without involving the CPU
suggestions.rationale: NCCL is tuned for GPU collectives; it avoids PCIe entirely for intra-node and saturates the NIC for inter-node

suggestions.action: If the reduction result is a scalar, perform the reduction on-device and transfer only the scalar result to the host
suggestions.mechanism: Use a device-side reduction kernel (e.g., cub::DeviceReduce) followed by a single 4-byte D2H transfer; then use MPI_Allreduce on the scalar
suggestions.rationale: Reduces PCIe traffic from O(N) to O(1) per rank; the MPI collective operates on tiny host scalars

mpi_halo_exchange.display_name: Inter-node halo exchange with host staging
nsys_signature: Per-halo-exchange triplet across the network: D2H cudaMemcpy → MPI_Sendrecv (inter-node) → H2D cudaMemcpy
GPU idle during MPI_Sendrecv; two full-halo PCIe transfers (D2H send-side + H2D receive-side) visible per exchange
MPI_Sendrecv duration includes both network latency and the time the remote rank spends staging; visible as a long CPU event in the MPI row

suggestions.action: Use CUDA-aware MPI with GPU-Direct RDMA so the Slingshot NIC reads/writes device memory directly without host staging
suggestions.mechanism: Pass device pointers to MPI_Sendrecv; set MPICH_GPU_SUPPORT_ENABLED=1 and MPICH_RDMA_ENABLED_CUDA=1; GTL uses GDRCopy for the RDMA path
suggestions.rationale: Eliminates both PCIe round-trips; the NIC DMAs directly from/to GPU HBM via GDRCopy; CPU is completely off the data path

suggestions.action: If GPU-Direct RDMA is unavailable, use double-buffering to overlap host staging of the next halo with computation on the current halo
suggestions.mechanism: Alternate between two device and two host halo buffers; issue D2H for buffer B while computing with the data just received in buffer A
suggestions.rationale: Hides the staging latency (D2H + network + H2D) behind useful GPU compute; amortizes the PCIe cost

suggestions.action: Reduce halo size or communication frequency by increasing the subdomain-to-surface ratio (stronger scaling in the volume dimension)
suggestions.mechanism: Use larger per-rank problem volumes so the ratio of compute work to halo size increases
suggestions.rationale: Arithmetic intensity of the communication pattern improves; the same network bandwidth serves more compute work
